Reviews.Author: 1knfzmbwll
Reviews.Comment: After using RbxCli for a month or so, It is my favourite external having features others dont.

Categories.Comment: Aimbot has many settings allowing me to configure it just the way i want, however unfortunately Sticky Aim and Dynamic FOV isnt the greatest.
The visuals are very clean but the chams tank the performance ALLOT

The ui is by fav my favourite out of all externals, One thing i dont like is how small the window is, Yes you can resize it but the cards inside the ui remain small with no indecator that you can scroll down in them.
It updates very fast
Rbxcli has many features other externals dont have like a very nicely made npc finding system, and a button to delete anything from game

Reviews.Author: s5gw2tsiq
Reviews.Comment: For $12 and it being in beta I'd say it's a good choice especially when considering how well optimized it is and easy to setup and how promising the LuaVM looks.

Categories.Comment: The sticky aim ignores distance check but the visible check/raycast is really well made.
Really good chams just make sure to enable cookie reading in settings, only issue I have is how skeletons look but it's in beta so I won't be too harsh.
Unique ui and is easy to navigate once you get used to it.
It's only ever had downtime when the auth was updating and it updates frequently.
It has a really smooth explorer and one of the few externals I've seen with an npc system, it doesn't have the most unique features but the features it currently does have is well refined.

Reviews.Author: 1bytmyho5w
Reviews.Comment:
Developers are friendly and interact with the community a lot, also very open to suggestions for new features/changes)
(There's also a lot of potential with the GC and Hybrid features, but I'm not experienced with either)

Categories.Comment: Average features, they get the job done but missing a lot of features compared to other externals. (mainly PF silent aim, multi point, and X/Y offset)

Boxes are very big for no reason (even with the Bounding Box option, there's just huge gaps),
Text should align with the top/bottom/sides of the bounding box, just doesn't look good when the name/any text is covering the box,
Chams are stuck on one color, imo an outline option would make this great.

Overall I love this interface, it's very easy to navigate, and looks great. My only issue is not having a section for running scripts as copy and pasting your script for every iteration is pretty annoying.
Developers are very active when it comes to updates, usually under 2 hours after Roblox updates, bug fixes are usually within the day and they compensate for downtime.

The LUA environment is genuinely top tier, tons of instances & their properties are supported, memory library for anything that isn't supported, stability & performance is pretty good (crashes happen, but the developers fix them if reported)

Reviews.Author: 27fnrarg4l
Reviews.Comment: goated external, scripting feels like home

Categories.Comment: hella good for a subscription-based external, has good aimbot with mouse mode or camera mode and customizable, but silent aim doesn't work on all games most of the time :(
visuals is good has everything u need for an external, had bad experience from it few weeks ago but it was due to my gpu driver configuration throttling the overlay (just disable Radeon Chill on AMD)
they have a unique twist for an overlay interface, i love it but there's no theming support for it though
coercion makes them very very fast to update

the scripting experience is what I really look into using externals and what I can say that it feels like home, I'm glad they using Luau for for interpreting those scripts and it became more goated when they added support for probing Roblox Luau garbage collection references (getgc thing for external)

they support many games but u can add support yourselves too with the entity api they provided 🔥❤️‍🔥

Reviews.Author: 6sdo50blm
Reviews.Comment: RbxCli is an great solution that clearly has potential for development.

Categories.Comment:
RbxCli, unlike its competitors, has quite decent targeting methods, including Mouse, Camera, and SilentAim. I was surprised by features like CamLock and several SilentAim methods; I consider this a huge advantage for customization.

There are issues with the Randomization and Smoothness settings, where we get slightly strange results like a spring effect when targeting; I don't think this is the intended result.

I'd like to specifically mention RbxCli's optimization, which is very well done. However, there are still moments when visual effects lag, for example, when sharing a screen, or when recording via GeForce Experience, and in some cases during regular gaming at 165 fps.

RbxCli still shows the best performance, in my opinion, compared to other products, but it still needs improvement.

You can create your own shaders for visual effects. Simply put, you can make your own style, RbxCli just gives you the basics.

I'd also like to note that Chameleon (Chams) is very well-designed in RbxCli

As far as I know, the RbxCli interface was purchased because the lead developer doesn't want or like to develop interfaces himself. This is understandable, but I'd really like to see a new interface design as soon as possible. It doesn't necessarily require a complete overhaul, as the current one is already good and somewhat ties in with Luau.

A good idea would be to hold an event among participants where they could suggest their own versions of the new interface.

It would be enough to give the UI a more modern and attractive look.
Claude might just be able to help with this.
RbxCli has its own Dumper for Roblox, as I know, updates happen almost instantly.

RbxCli impresses with its LuauVM, along with its Hybrid mode, which even allows for instance-based work like RemoteEvent, ProximityPrompt, and TouchInterest. It also supports raknet and trivial features like GC and Memory libraries.

Overall, Luau in RbxCli is well-implemented and moving in a great direction.
